title: Price makes program history with century mark on mat
content: On Feb. 4, at the 5A South Regional Tournament in Texarkana against Arkadelphia, Bauxite senior wrestler Jacklyn Price reached a milestone of 100 wins, becoming the first female wrestler to achieve the century mark in Bauxite history. “It feels really good, especially being the first female wrestler to achieve 100 wins in Bauxite history and doing it in only three seasons,” Price said. Price said the milestone reflects the hard work she has put into the sport. “I think it shows that hard work pays off, and if you work hard, you can achieve anything you want,” Price said. Price expressed that the coaching staff was essential in helping her succeed. “Coach Johnson has been with the team since I joined, and he puts not just me but the whole team in a position to win,” Price said. “He treats us all like bonus family and makes sure we all have what we need to be successful.” Price also praised assistant coach Jennifer Hilderbrand, who joined the program this year. “Coach Hilderbrand was a new addition to the team this year and stepped up for us tremendously,” Price said. “Learning as much as she could and cheering us on whether we were winning or losing, and her husband, Rob, was a great helper to us as well. Both coaches gave up many weekends and weeknights to be with us, and we wouldn’t be the program we are without them.” Price said the bond with her teammates has also played an important role throughout the season. “Wrestling isn’t like many other sports where boys and girls are separated,” Price said. “Every day we practice together, so it’s like one big family. They’ve always believed in me, and I’ve always believed in them.” She said wrestling has grown into something that means a great deal to her. “It started as something I did because Coach Johnson asked me to, and it became a community and a family, not just with my team but with other wrestlers I met along the way. It’s going to be hard to let go as a senior,” she said. Bauxite head coach Russell Johnson said the milestone reflects Price’s dedication. “100 wins is a huge milestone for high school wrestlers. This is not something easily obtained,” Johnson said. “Jacklyn has been a dominant force on the mat at 110 pounds since she first started her sophomore year. She has worked her tail off taking every match she could to achieve this. I’m beyond proud of the dedication and determination she has shown to reach such a goal, having never wrestled before her 10th-grade year.”
creator: keymon frazier keygotmedia com

title: Čitatelj ostao iznenađen računicom u Dubrovniku: ‘Čudo su prodavači povrća, trebalo bi mjeriti vagom za drogu...‘
content: Stigla nam je jutros poruka čitatelja koji je bio na gruškoj placi te ostao iznenađen odnosom gramaže i cijene odnosno računanjem na balice. - Sad sam bio na placi u Gružu. Balica mladog luka je 1 euro, isto tako i petrusin. Trebalo bi mjeriti vagom za drogu jer balica nema 5 grama, znači kilo je više od 20 eura. Čudo su prodavači povrća na placi! Ispada da je kilo freških velikih bukava za 10 eura jeftino, a to je prije par godina bilo 20-30 kuna. Balice, balice... - objasnio je čitatelj.

title: Meta unveils plans for batch of in-house AI chips
content: self.__next_f.push([1,"\u003cp\u003eMeta Platforms on Wednesday unveiled a roadmap of four new chips that the company is making in-house, as it rapidly expands its data centers.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cad id=\"ad-1\"\u003e\u003c/ad\u003e\u003cp\u003eLike many big tech companies such as Alphabet and Microsoft, Meta has invested heavily in building a team that can design chips in-house in addition to purchasing off-the-shelf products made by Nvidia and Advanced Micro Devices.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cad id=\"ad-2\"\u003e\u003c/ad\u003e\u003cp\u003eMaking chips designed to tackle the specific types of data crunching Meta requires can lead to designs that use less energy and at a better cost.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cad id=\"ad-3\"\u003e\u003c/ad\u003e\u003cp\u003eThe new chips are part of the company’s Meta Training and Inference Accelerator (MTIA) program and the first of the new chips called the MTIA 300 is in use powering the company’s ranking and recommendation systems. The other three will be rolled out this year and in 2027, with the final two chips, the MTIA 450 and 500 being designed to perform inference, the process when an AI model such as the one that powers the ChatGPT app responds to customer queries and requests.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cad id=\"ad-4\"\u003e\u003c/ad\u003e\u003cp\u003e“We see inference demand exploding at the moment and that’s what we’re currently focused on,” Yee Jiun Song, Meta’s vice president of engineering, said in an interview.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cad id=\"ad-5\"\u003e\u003c/ad\u003e\u003cp\u003eMeta has had some success with inference chips but has struggled with its long-time ambitions to make a generative AI training chip, capable of building the large models that power AI apps.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cad id=\"ad-6\"\u003e\u003c/ad\u003e\u003cp\u003eBeginning with the MTIA 400, which the company says is on the path to being used in its data centers, Meta has designed an entire system around the chips, which is roughly the size of several server racks and includes a version of liquid cooling.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cad id=\"ad-7\"\u003e\u003c/ad\u003e\u003cp\u003eThe company plans to release the new chips at six-month intervals because it is rapidly expanding the number of data centers it uses to run apps like Instagram and Facebook, Song said.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cad id=\"ad-8\"\u003e\u003c/ad\u003e\u003cp\u003e“That is the reality of how quickly our infrastructure is being built out,” Song said.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cad...

title: AP Trending SummaryBrief at 10:19 a.m. EDT
content: Man taken into custody after driving van into security gate outside White House, authorities say WASHINGTON (AP) — Authorities say a man was taken into custody after driving his van into a security barrier at the White House, The Secret Service says the man crashed into the temporary security barrier Wednesday morning just before 6:30 a.m. He was immediately arrested by officers from the Secret Service’s uniformed division. The man's identity was not immediately released. He was being interviewed by investigators and criminal charges were pending. ‘Ship of Gold’ treasure hunter released from prison, but 500 gold coins remain unaccounted for A former deep-sea treasure hunter who spent more than a decade in prison after refusing to disclose the whereabouts of missing gold coins is now free. Federal records show Ohio-born research scientist Tommy Thompson was released from prison last Wednesday. Thompson made one of the great finds in American history in 1988 when he found what was known as the Ship of Gold off the coast of South Carolina. The wreckage held thousands of pounds of sunken treasure that sat at the bottom of the Atlantic Ocean for more than 150 years. A dispute over missing gold coins landed Thompson in prison while he contended he didn’t know their whereabouts. Italy buys Caravaggio painting for about $35 million, one of its largest payouts for a single work ROME (AP) — Italy's Culture Ministry says the government has bought a rare portrait by baroque painter Caravaggio for 30 million euros, one of the largest investments ever for a single artwork. The portrait, painted around 1598 and attributed to Caravaggio in 1963, depicts Maffeo Barberini, a nobleman who later became Pope Urban VIII. The painting was acquired from a private collection by the Italian state after over a year of negotiations, and will now enter Rome’s Palazzo Barberini permanent collection. The Portrait of Monsignor Maffeo Barberini depicts the future pope in his 30s, dressed as a cleric of the Apostolic Chamber, at a crucial moment in his rise to power. Wildflowers are blooming in the driest place in North America — but not for long DEATH VALLEY, Calif. (AP) — Visitors are flocking to Death Valley National Park in California and Nevada to take in a rare phenomenon known as a superbloom. He was being interviewed by investigators and criminal charges were pending. ‘Ship of Gold’ treasure hunter released from prison, but 500 gold coins remain unaccounted for A former deep-sea treasure hunter who spent more than a decade in prison after refusing to disclose the whereabouts of missing gold coins is now free. Federal records show Ohio-born research scientist Tommy Thompson was released from prison last Wednesday. Thompson made one of the great finds in American history in 1988 when he found what was known as the Ship of Gold off the coast of South Carolina. The wreckage held thousands of pounds of sunken treasure that sat at the bottom of the Atlantic Ocean for more than 150 years. A dispute over missing gold coins landed Thompson in prison while he contended he didn’t know their whereabouts. The portrait, painted around 1598 and attributed to Caravaggio in 1963, depicts Maffeo Barberini, a nobleman who later became Pope Urban VIII. The painting was acquired from a private collection by the Italian state after over a year of negotiations, and will now enter Rome’s Palazzo Barberini permanent collection. The Portrait of Monsignor Maffeo Barberini depicts the future pope in his 30s, dressed as a cleric of the Apostolic Chamber, at a crucial moment in his rise to power. title: Dr Amir's warning over 'silent' condition that can cause cancer
content: A doctor has cautioned that what you believe to be an allergy or cold might actually be a "silent" health condition that can lead to cancer in severe cases. Dr Amir Khan explained how to identify silent reflux. In a video shared on social media platform Instagram, Dr Amir provided more information about this lesser-known condition. Dr Amir, who is best recognised for his appearances on ITV , asked: "Are you constantly clearing your throat, have a horse voice, constant, sore throat, phlegm a feeling of something stuck in your throat or a wheeze that just won't settle? "Well, you might have something called silent reflux, let's get into it." He added: "Now, you may have heard of normal acid reflux when acid rises from the stomach into your food pipe or oesophagus, and causes that discomfort or burning sensation in the top of your tummy area or chest - heartburn. "But silent reflux, also known as laryngopharyngeal reflux, is different." According to Dr Amir, with this condition, the acid travels further up, beyond your food pipe. Rather than triggering heartburn and indigestion, it moves towards your voice box, your throat and your sinus area, irritating "all of them". This condition can often be overlooked or confused with something else. Dr Amir explained: "It's called silent reflux because it doesn't give you the typical heartburn symptoms." He then outlined some of the key indicators of silent reflux as: A hoarse voice The sensation of something in your throat A persistent cough or sore throat Post-nasal drip Wheeze Frequent upper respiratory tract infections He stated: "Remember, you might think you have allergies or a constant cold, but it could be this silent reflux. " There are several potential causes of silent reflux. "Now, you get silent reflux because there are problems with the sphincters or muscular valves whose job it is to stop acid from going beyond the stomach", Dr Amir explained. "You have one here at the bottom of your food pipe or oesophagus. If this one isn't working properly, acid rises up and causes heartburn. "But you also have a one at the top of your food pipe here, a second one. And if this one isn't working properly, the acid rises higher and causes all of these silent reflux symptoms. "Now our throat and sinus, and nose area don't have the same levels of protection against acid as our food pipe does, so we get symptoms." Factors that can trigger silent reflux include: Excessive alcohol consumption Smoking Being overweight...
creator: fiona callingham

title: EPRA Shuts Down Coastal Gas Facility
content: The Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA) has shut down an illegal Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) bulk storage facility in Mariakani, Kilifi County, over safety violations and a lack of a licence. The operation, conducted in the Shangia area of Mariakani, followed intelligence reports about the existence of an unlicensed LPG storage site operating within a densely populated area. EPRA’s Enforcement and Surveillance officers carried out the operation in collaboration with a multi-agency team from the National Police Service and Kilifi County government. According to EPRA, the team inspected the site on February 20, 2026, before sealing it off and reporting the matter at Mariakani Police Station. EPRA revealed that the facility had been constructed and operated without the necessary construction permits and operating licences required under petroleum sector regulations. “The facility was constructed and operated without the requisite construction permits and operational licences from the Authority, posing a significant safety risk to the surrounding community,” EPRA said in a statement. EPRA also expressed concern over the facility’s location, noting that it had been set up within a busy commercial area close to schools, a mosque, a dispensary, and a major highway with heavy traffic. The energy regulator warned that the potential consequences of a fire or gas explosion in such an environment could be catastrophic. “Such facilities are owned by profiteers who operate without regard to the dire consequences their operations can cause to the public, including the risk of mass casualties in the event of a fire or gas explosion,” the Authority said. To further add to the danger, the facility was also located near a community water point, directly opposite a power substation, and adjacent to a high-voltage electricity transmission line. The regulator revealed that despite being issued an official decommissioning notice that was visibly displayed at the facility’s entrance, the owner failed to surrender the premises to regulators. “The owner will face legal consequences for constructing an LPG bulk storage facility without a valid construction permit and for operating an unlicensed gas storage and handling facility contrary to the Petroleum Act,” EPRA stated. The regulator reiterated its commitment to enforcing petroleum sector regulations and ensuring LPG infrastructure across the country adheres to strict safety and regulatory standards.
creator: timothy cerullo

title: Man taken into custody after driving van into security gate outside White House, authorities say
content: WASHINGTON (AP) — A man was taken into custody on Wednesday after driving his van into a security barrier outside the White House, authorities said. The Secret Service said the man crashed into the temporary security barrier just before 6:30 a.m. He was immediately arrested by officers from the Secret Service’s uniformed division, the agency said. The man, whose identity was not immediately released, was being interviewed by investigators. Criminal charges were pending, the Secret Service said. A police bomb squad was called to the scene, checked the vehicle and determined it to be safe.
creator: the associated press

title: Iran withdraws from 2026 World Cup over conflict with US-Israel
content: Iran has announced that it will not be taking part in the upcoming 2026 FIFA World Cup. This was made known by the country’s sports minister, Ahmad Donyamali, who said that there are “no conditions” under which the national team can take part in the tournament amid escalating conflict with the United States and Israel. The Sports Minister said the decision follows recent military strikes by the United States and Israel that killed Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ali Khamenei, and triggered a wider regional conflict. “Since this corrupt government assassinated our leader, we have no conditions under which we can participate in the World Cup,” Donyamali said in a televised interview. “In view of the malicious measures taken against Iran, two wars were forced upon us within eight or nine months, and several thousand of our people were killed. Therefore, we definitely have no possibility of participating in this way,” he added. The World Cup is scheduled to take place from June 11 to July 19 and will be jointly hosted by the United States, Canada, and Mexico. Iran had already qualified for the tournament after finishing top of Group A in the Asian qualifiers and had been drawn in a group with Belgium, Egypt, and New Zealand. Their group-stage matches were scheduled to take place in Los Angeles and Seattle in the United States. Earlier, the president of FIFA, Gianni Infantino, said he had received assurances from U.S. President Donald Trump that the Iranian team would be welcome to compete in the tournament despite rising geopolitical tensions. Infantino noted that the World Cup should serve as an event that brings nations together, saying global football has a role in promoting unity during difficult times. However, Iranian officials maintain that the current security and political situation makes participation impossible, raising uncertainty over whether the team will ultimately withdraw and which country might replace it in the tournament lineup.
creator: william narh

title: مشکلی در تامین برق وجود ندارد
content: به گزارش گروه رسانهای شرق، سرپرست معاونت امور برق و انرژی وزیر نیرو گفت: روند تامین برق تداوم داشته و فعلا مشکلی وجود ندارد. مصطفی رجبی مشهدی روز چهارشنبه ضمن تسلیت شهادت مولای متقیان ادامه داد: وضعیت فعلی شبکه کشکر پایدار است. وی افزود: اکنون پیک شبانه ما بیش از ۴۲ تا ۴۳ هزار مگاوات و پیک روز در حدود ۳۸ هزار مگاوات است. معاون وزیر نیرو خاطرنشانکرد: همه همکاران صنعت برق در حالت آماده باش قرار دارند و آماده خدمت رسانی به مردم عزیز هستند. رجبی مشهدی گفت: فقط در تهران بزرگ ما بیش از ۱۷۰ حادثه ناشی از حملات دشمن داشتیم و در بقیه نقاط کشور هم متناسب با حملاتی که انجام شده، شبکه های برق دچار آسیب دیدگی شده است که همکاران ما با حضور به موقع توانستد معایب را برطرف کنند. وی خاطرنشانکرد: در حال حاضر همه مشترکین تامین برق می شوند و خاموشی ناشی از حوادث مربوط به جنگ نداریم و روند تامین برق طبیعی است. معاون برق و انرژی وزیر نیرو از مردم خواست از نزدیک شدن به نقاطی که شبکه آسیب می بیند خودداری کنند زیرا ممکن است سیم ها برق داشته و خطرناک باشند. رجبی مشهدی همچنین نسبت به رعایت مدیریت مصرف در این شرایط تاکید کرد و از مردم خواست صرفه جویی را همچنان اعمال کنند.
creator: شبکه شرق

title: Forensic team identifies 12 new dictatorship victims at Córdoba’s ‘La Perla’ site
content: A federal court in Córdoba confirmed Tuesday that experts have identified the remains of 12 victims of Argentina’s last military dictatorship (1976–1983) previously unknown to investigators. The remains – including a femur, skull fragments and other bones – were discovered within the grounds of the La Calera military garrison, where the clandestine detention centre ‘La Perla’ once operated in Córdoba Province. The fragments were uncovered during excavations led by the Equipo Argentino de Antropología Forense (Argentine Forensic Anthropology Team, EAAF), the internationally renowned team that identifies victims using DNA testing and forensic analysis of skeletal remains. The team of experts used ground-penetrating radar technology and soil analysis to detect unnatural earth movements before excavations began. According to a statement released by Federal Court No. 3 of Córdoba, the 12 victims were identified through “anthropological and genetic analyses carried out by the EAAF on skeletal remains recovered at the La Calera Military Garrison,” where the La Perla detention centre once operated. The discoveries were made in an area of mass graves within the grounds that belonged to the Third Army Corps. The court overseeing the case must first inform the victims’ relatives of the findings. Only afterwards – and if the families consent – will the identities of those recovered be made public. Sources with knowledge of the case say both male and female remains were found. Forensic tests indicate the victims had injuries that were consistent with gunshot wounds. The remains were discovered in September 2025 following excavations on a site known as Loma del Torito, part of the La Calera military garrison. The excavations were carried out partly in response to a complaint filed nearly 40 years ago by human rights activist and Nobel Peace Prize laureate Adolfo Pérez Esquivel and relatives of the disappeared. The positive ID comes as Argentina prepares to mark the 50th anniversary of the March 24, 1976 coup d’état that brought the military junta to power. Human rights organisations estimate that around 30,000 people were forcibly disappeared during the dictatorship. But since taking office in December 2023, officials in President Javier Milei’s government have repeatedly questioned that figure. Every March 24, hundreds of thousands of people take to the streets across Argentina to demand justice for the victims and information about their fate – details that...
creator: james grainger

title: Police constable and son of Lancashire Police Assistant Chief Constable charged with 14 child sex offences
content: Response officer PC Thomas Winstanley appeared at Birmingham Crown Court on February 12 charged with 14 offences against a minor. The 25-year-old, of Eccleston near Chorley, was charged with: three counts of rape; two counts of sexual assault; two counts of causing or inciting a girl to engage in non-penetrative sexual activity; two counts of engaging in non-penetrative sexual activity with a girl; two counts of sexual communication with a child; one count of sexual assaulting a girl by penetration with part of body / a thing; one count of engaging in sexual activity in presence of a child and one count of possessing a prohibited image of a child. During the short hearing at Birmingham Crown Court Winstanley, who was suspended and then subsequently handed in his resignation to Lancashire Constabulary, entered a not guilty plea to all the charges. It is understood his resignation has not formally being confirmed. An independent investigation into the alleged allegations was held by Cumbria Constabulary following his first arrest in October 2024. He was bailed to appear again at Birmingham Crown Court for a further ground rules hearing on June 19. A provisional trial date of May 8, 2028 has been listed by the judge. Thomas Winstanley, who was posted in Leyland before his arrest and suspension, is the son of serving Assistant Chief Constable Mark Winstanley of Lancashire Constabulary. Assistant Chief Constable Mark Winstanley is a Gold Public Order Commander and Strategic Firearms Commander. As part of his role he is responsible for Crime and Specialist Operations across Lancashire, as well as having force responsibility for Investigations. A Lancashire Police spokesman said: “We can confirm a Lancashire officer has been charged with a number of sexual offences. “Thomas Winstanley is a response PC who was based in our South division. He was first arrested in October 2024 and immediately suspended following his arrest. “The investigation has been managed independently by Cumbria Police. “Thomas Winstanley has tendered his resignation from Lancashire Constabulary. “Due to legal proceedings, we aren't able to offer further comment at this time.” Lancashire Police referred the case to Cumbria Constabulary in late 2024 to lead the investigation to ensure public confidence in a transparent and independent process.
creator: vanessa sims

title: ‘We dodged a bullet’: Putnam, La Salle counties escape overnight storms with little reported damage
content: There were two tornado warnings sounded Tuesday in Putnam County . One was for a funnel cloud spotted in neighboring Marshall County, the other for a rotating cloud spotted north of Granville. But Putnam County officials heaved sighs of relief after the storms passed. The twister in Marshall County didn’t stray across the county line and the rotating cloud didn’t produce a touchdown. “We dodged a bullet,” Putnam County Sheriff Josh Boedigheimer reported Wednesday morning. Boedigheimer confirmed the two warnings issued, but reported no storm damage of any note. There were no reported injuries, structural damage, or blackouts. The National Weather Service in Davenport largely confirmed that. A meteorologist said the office is still collecting data from a broad region hit by the storms. Still, so far, there are no indications of serious storm damage in either Putnam or Bureau County. “We know of hail in PC and BC, but we don’t have any reports of wind or tornado damage as of yet,” meteorologist Matt Friedlein said. Officials in neighboring counties were breathing easier, as well. Fred Moore, director of La Salle County Emergency Management Agency, reported Wednesday that his office was alerted only to “minor” tree damage and wires down north of Ottawa. While there was one EMS dispatch, Moore said, the medical call was not related to the storm. “We skated by relatively unharmed,” Moore said, “and I’m quite happy with that.”
creator: shaw local news network

title: Tesco F&F releases stylish and flattering midi skirt perfect that shoppers 'love'
content: Sometimes your day might be so busy that you are rushing between work and social events meaning you need outfits that are great for both the office and going out with friends. If you are looking for some new pieces that are versatile, you might want to take a trip to Tesco to check out its clothes. The shop's clothing brand F&F has just released the F&F A-Line Full Midi Skirt in Chocolate that can easily be styled for both the office and meetings with friends. The flowy skirt is "a must-have in your cool-weather styles" and is cut to a "flattering" midi length. The skirt can be paired with a simple fitted top or an oversized jumper along with some heeled boots to create a perfectly put-together look. The skirt is currently being sold for £25 and will likely be a staple in your spring and summer wardrobe. The skirt was shared to the Tesco F&F Instagram page, which currently has 886K followers, along with some other springtime pieces. The post was captioned: "Tuesday morning fit check. @lifeof_kayley_" Fans of the Tesco clothing brand have taken to social media to share their opinions on the skirt and other F&F items. One commenter said: "Love it." If you want to get your hands on the skirt, you can go to the Tesco website to browse all of its clothes. You could also go to a Tesco store in-person with a clothing department. Boden has some alternative options that you might like if this midi skirt is not quite what you are looking for. There is this Tulle Tiered Skirt that you might like if you are looking for something a bit brighter in your wardrobe or this Layla Linen Skirt, which is a light choice for the summer. New Look also has some pieces in stock for the new season. There is this Brown Gingham Seersucker Mini Skirt that will be cute for spring or this Brown Satin Polka Dot Lace Hem Midi Skirt, which has an adorable lace trim for a stylish finish.
creator: amy britton

title: ‘We were exposed’
content: DAILY SUN PHOTO Pakistan head coach Mike Hesson admitted his side’s batting was “exposed” after a disappointing performance led to a heavy defeat against Bangladesh in the opening ODI at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium on Wednesday. Pakistan were bowled out for just 114 before Bangladesh chased the target comfortably, and Hesson did not hide his frustration with the visitors’ display. “It was a pretty disappointing batting performance. I don’t think there’s anything shying away from that,” Hesson said after the match. “And it wasn’t just the youngsters. We were exposed to some good bowling and then we went into our shell a little bit.” The head coach acknowledged that Pakistan’s batting struggled to cope with the pressure created by Bangladesh’s bowlers and said the team must respond quickly ahead of the next match in the series. “So we have to turn things around really quickly,” he said. Pakistan fielded several young players - four debutants - in the match, but Hesson stressed that development comes through difficult experiences and that the newcomers must learn from the setback. “Youngsters have got to learn and you have to give them opportunities to learn and grow,” he said. “We would love them to run out and score a hundred on debut and things like that, but they have to go through some tough challenges.” Hesson urged his players to show resilience in the coming days as Pakistan attempt to bounce back in the series. “We have to show some resilience over the next few days and come back and fight well in the next game,” he said. The coach also admitted Pakistan struggled to apply pressure with the ball while defending a below-par total, saying the bowlers tried too hard to find breakthroughs. “When you’re defending a well below-par total, even if you want to be disciplined and create pressure, you do go searching a little bit,” Hesson explained. “
creator: daily sun report

title: Bidar | ಸೌಹಾರ್ದತೆ ಬಲಪಡಿಸಲು ಇಫ್ತಾರ್ ಕೂಟ ಸಹಾಯಕ: ಉಮಾಕಾಂತ್ ವಿಳಸಪೂರೆ
content: ಬೀದರ್: ರಮಝಾನ್‌ ಹಿನ್ನೆಲೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಸೌಹಾರ್ದತೆ, ಸಹೋದರತ್ವ ಹಾಗೂ ಪರಸ್ಪರ ಗೌರವವನ್ನು ಬಲಪಡಿಸುವ ಉದ್ದೇಶದಿಂದ ಇಫ್ತಾರ್ ಕೂಟಗಳನ್ನು ಆಯೋಜಿಸಲಾಗುತ್ತದೆ ಎಂದು ಉಮಾಕಾಂತ್ ವಿಳಸಪೂರೆ ಹೇಳಿದರು. ಔರಾದ್ ತಾಲೂಕಿನ ಬೋರ್ಗಿ ಗ್ರಾಮದಲ್ಲಿ ಮಂಗಳವಾರ ಸಂಜೆ ನಡೆದ ಸೌಹಾರ್ದತೆಯ ಇಫ್ತಾರ್ ಕೂಟದಲ್ಲಿ ಮಾತನಾಡಿದ ಅವರು, ಸರ್ವಧರ್ಮದ ತತ್ವಸಾರವನ್ನು ತಿಳಿದುಕೊಳ್ಳಲು ಇಂತಹ ಕಾರ್ಯಕ್ರಮಗಳು ಅಗತ್ಯವೆಂದು ತಿಳಿಸಿದರು. ಗ್ರಾಮದಲ್ಲಿ ಹಿಂದಿನಿಂದಲೂ ಎಲ್ಲಾ ಸಮುದಾಯದವರು ಶಾಂತಿ, ಸೌಹಾರ್ದತೆ ಮತ್ತು ಸಹೋದರತ್ವದಿಂದ ಬದುಕುತ್ತಿರುವ ಪರಂಪರೆ ಇದೆ. ಆ ಪರಂಪರೆಯನ್ನು ಮುಂದುವರಿಸುವ ಜವಾಬ್ದಾರಿ ಯುವಕರ ಮೇಲಿದೆ ಎಂದರು. ಈ ವೇಳೆ ಲಿಂಗಾಯತ ಸಮನ್ವಯ ಸಮಿತಿ ರಾಜ್ಯ ಸಂಚಾಲಕ ಶ್ರೀಕಾಂತ ಸ್ವಾಮಿ ಮಾತನಾಡಿ, ಸಮಾಜದಲ್ಲಿ ಶಾಂತಿ, ಸೌಹಾರ್ದತೆ ಹಾಗೂ ಪರಸ್ಪರ ಗೌರವವನ್ನು ಬೆಳೆಸಲು ಸರ್ವಧರ್ಮಗಳ ತತ್ವಸಾರವನ್ನು ತಿಳಿದುಕೊಳ್ಳುವುದು ಅಗತ್ಯ. ವಿಭಿನ್ನ ಧರ್ಮಗಳ ಸಾರವೂ ಒಂದೇ ಮಾನವೀಯ ಮೌಲ್ಯಗಳನ್ನು ಸಾರುತ್ತದೆ ಎಂಬುದನ್ನು ಜನರಿಗೆ ತಿಳಿಸಲು ಇಂತಹ ಸೌಹಾರ್ದ ಕೂಟಗಳು ಮುಖ್ಯಪಾತ್ರ ವಹಿಸುತ್ತವೆ ಎಂದು ಹೇಳಿದರು. ಗ್ರಾಮದ ಮುಖಂಡ ಅಯೂಬ್ ಖಾನ್ ಪಾಟೀಲ್ ಮಾತನಾಡಿ, ರಮಝಾನ್‌ ಮುಸ್ಲಿಮರ ಪವಿತ್ರ ತಿಂಗಳಾಗಿದ್ದು ಅಲ್ಲಾಹನನ್ನು ಸಾಕ್ಷಾತ್ಕರಿಸಿಕೊಳ್ಳುವ ಮಾರ್ಗವಾಗಿದೆ. ಇದು ಕೋಮು ಸೌಹಾರ್ದದ ಸಂಕೇತವಾಗಿದ್ದು ಎಲ್ಲರೂ ಸೇರಿ ಹಬ್ಬಗಳನ್ನು ಆಚರಿಸಿದರೆ ಸಮಾಜದಲ್ಲಿ ಶಾಂತಿ ಮತ್ತು ನೆಮ್ಮದಿ ನೆಲೆಸಲಿದೆ ಎಂದು ಅಭಿಪ್ರಾಯಪಟ್ಟರು. ಈ ಸಂದರ್ಭದಲ್ಲಿ ಸಾಮಾಜಿಕ ಕಾರ್ಯಕರ್ತ ಜಗದೀಶ್ವರ್ ಬಿರಾದಾರ್, ಸಂಗಮೇಶ್ ಕೌಟಗೆ, ಚನ್ನಪ್ಪಾ ಬಿರಾದಾರ್, ವಿಶ್ವನಾಥ್ ಬುಣಗೆ, ಶಾಮರಾವ್ ಜೈನಾಪುರೆ, ಶೇಖ್ ಖಲಿಲ್, ಸಿದ್ದಪ್ಪಾ ಉಜ್ಜಣಗೆ ಮತ್ತು ಗ್ರಾಮದ ಸರ್ವ ಸಮುದಾಯದ ಹಿರಿಯರು, ಮುಖಂಡರು ಉಪಸ್ಥಿತರಿದ್ದರು.
creator: ವಾರ್ತಾಭಾರತಿ

title: RCB to host Sunrisers Hyderabad in opener as IPL 2026 to commence on March 28
content: Mumbai, March 11, 2026 The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) on Wednesday announced the schedule for the first phase of the Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026, to be played from March 28 to April 12, 2026. “As three states are scheduled to undergo State Assembly elections during this period, the full schedule of the tournament will be announced once the poll dates are announced,” BCCI said in a statement. The 19th edition of the biggest T20 league on the globe will commence with defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) hosting Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) at the M. Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru. A total of 20 matches will be played across 10 venues: Bengaluru, Mumbai, Guwahati, New Chandigarh, Lucknow, Kolkata, Chennai, Delhi, Ahmedabad and Hyderabad. During this period, the tournament will feature four double-headers, with the afternoon matches beginning at 03:30 PM IST and the evening matches commencing at 07:30 PM IST. Following the opening encounter on Saturday, Mumbai Indians will take on Kolkata Knight Riders at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai on Sunday. The first double-header of the season will take place on April 4, 2026, when Delhi Capitals face Mumbai Indians in the afternoon match at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in Delhi, followed by Gujarat Titans taking on Rajasthan Royals at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. Rajasthan Royals will begin their home campaign in Guwahati, where they will host Chennai Super Kings and Mumbai Indians. Punjab Kings (PBKS), meanwhile, will play their early home fixtures at the PCA Stadium in New Chandigarh. During the season, Royal Challengers Bengaluru will play five home matches in Bengaluru and two in Raipur. Punjab Kings will play four home matches in New Chandigarh and three in Dharamshala, while Rajasthan Royals will play three home matches in Guwahati and four in Jaipur. BCCI also said that “The matches scheduled in Bengaluru are subject to clearance from the Expert Committee constituted by the Government of Karnataka. The committee will conduct a meeting and inspection of the M. Chinnaswamy Stadium on March 13, 2026, during which a full-scale mock demonstration of match-day arrangements will be carried out to assess the stadium’s preparedness for hosting IPL matches.”(Agency) The post RCB to host Sunrisers Hyderabad in opener as IPL 2026 to commence on March 28 appeared first on Yes Punjab News .

title: Cinco empleos sin título universitario que mejor pagan en Uruguay en 2026
WNBA, players union conclude overnight meeting without new CBA
content: A meeting between WNBA representatives and the players association ended early Wednesday morning without a deal on a new collective bargaining agreement, multiple outlets reported. The meeting, held at a New York hotel, began at 5 p.m. Tuesday and ended about 12 hours later, ESPN reported. League officials had set March 10 as the deadline for a new CBA agreement to avoid the loss of regular-season games. The league and the players have been at a standstill for months, with revenue sharing and housing among the key issues. The regular season is scheduled to begin May 8. "It's complex," WNBA commissioner Cathy Englebert told reporters outside the hotel after 5 a.m. "We're working towards a win-win deal like we've been saying, a transformational deal for these players that balances all the things we've been trying to balance with continued investment by our owners. So we're working hard towards that, and we still have work to do." Women's National Basketball Players Association executive director Terri Jackson said "conversations are continuing, and they need to be," ESPN reported. "Every meeting is a positive meeting," Jackson said, per ESPN. "Seriously, every meeting is a positive meeting. The fact that we scheduled meetings, that we offer dates to schedule meetings that we actually get together, get in the room. I think that's positive. It's taking as long as it's taking. But you know, that's what it needs to be." The league has not revealed whether it has flexibility to start the season on time with the deadline passed. The WNBA draft is scheduled for April 13, with training camps opening six days later. The league also must hold a free agency period, an expansion draft with the Toronto Tempo and Portland Fire set to begin play this upcoming season, as well as preseason games. The players have been without a collective bargaining agreement since they opted out of their existing agreement in October 2024, a year before its Oct. 31, 2025, expiration, with hopes of having a new deal in place last fall. title: Rihanna Jets Out of LA After Armed Suspect Targets Her Beverly Hills Mansion
content: Pop superstar Rihanna has fled Los Angeles on a private jet following a terrifying shooting attempt at her Beverly Hills mansion that saw an armed stalker fire a barrage of bullets while her young children were inside. The 'Diamonds' singer was spotted boarding a flight at Van Nuys Airport on Monday, less than 24 hours after 35-year-old Ivanna Lisette Ortiz allegedly opened fire on the property with a rifle. Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman confirmed that Rihanna, her partner A$AP Rocky, their three children—RZA, Riot, and infant daughter Rocki—and Rihanna's mother were all at the estate during the Sunday afternoon ambush. While the couple was reportedly in a trailer on the grounds, at least one round pierced a wall of the main residence where the children were located. Ortiz, a licensed speech pathologist from Florida, now faces 14 felony charges, including attempted murder and 10 counts of assault with a semiautomatic firearm. Shooting Outside Beverly Hills Mansion According to the Los Angeles Police Department, officers responded to reports of gunfire at the singer's residence at around 1:15 pm local time (9:15 pm GMT) on 8 March. Investigators say a suspect fired several shots at the property using what authorities described as an AR-15-style rifle. Police said between five and seven rounds were discharged toward the gate of the home from across the street. Dispatch audio referenced as many as 10 shots during the incident. At the time of the shooting, Rihanna, whose full name is Robyn Rihanna Fenty, was inside the house with other individuals. Despite the alarming circumstances, authorities confirmed that no injuries were reported. The suspect, identified as Ivanna Lisette Ortiz , was later taken into custody without incident and booked on suspicion of attempted murder. She is reportedly being held on multi-million-dollar bail while awaiting further court proceedings. Rihanna Seen Leaving Los Angeles Rihanna reportedly leaves her LA home. X/@WhizBuddy In the hours following the arrest, the singer was reportedly heading to an airport in the Los Angeles area before boarding a small private jet. Images circulating online showed luggage being loaded into several vehicles outside the property before the convoy departed for the airport. It remains unclear whether Rihanna had pre-existing travel plans or whether her departure was connected to the shooting incident. However, reports suggest the decision to leave the city came shortly after...

title: The Latest: Global energy concerns mount as Iran threats stop cargo through the Strait of Hormuz
content: The ongoing Israeli-American war on Iran, for all its complexity and global effects, boils down to a single question: Who can take the pain the longest? A surge in oil prices points to what may be Iran’s most effective weapon and the United States’ biggest vulnerability in continuing the campaign: Damaging the world economy. Wednesday’s major developments include Iranian attacks against commercial ships across the Persian Gulf and its targeting of Dubai International Airport, escalating a campaign of squeezing the oil-rich region as global energy concerns mount. The U.S. campaign of air strikes also continues, and the Israeli military said it is simultaneously striking across Iran as well as Hezbollah infrastructure in Beirut. The war has killed at least 1,230 people in Iran, at least 570 in Lebanon and 12 in Israel, according to officials in those countries. The Pentagon said Tuesday that about 140 U.S. service members have been wounded, with 108 of them back on duty. Eight suffered severe injuries and seven have been killed. An Israeli intelligence assessment indicates that Iran’s new supreme leader also was wounded at the start of the war. Here is the latest: Putin speaks with UAE leader Russia’s President Vladimir Putin spoke about the war Wednesday with the United Arab Emirates president, Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan. The Kremlin said in a readout that the leaders “continued their exchange of views on the dangerously deteriorating situation in the Middle East, which has grave consequences for Iran and the Arab countries.” In his second call with Al Nahyan since the start of the Iran war, Putin emphasized “the need to stop the further escalation of the conflict and settle it through talks.” He also thanked Al Nahyan for offering assistance and support to Russian citizens in the UAE. Monitors report a dozen incidents involving vessels since war began At least 12 incidents have been confirmed involving vessels in and around the Strait of Hormuz since the war began, according to two global trackers. The International Maritime Organization reports 12 involving merchant vessels as of Wednesday. IMO secretary-general Arsenio Dominguez earlier said at least seven mariners had been killed. And the United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations center, run by the British military, says it has received 17 reports of incidents affecting vessels in and around the Arabian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz and Gulf of Oman as of Wednesday. It lists 13 attacks and four others...

title: “I think we never want it to happen again,” says Ulster Rugby’s Michael Lowry.
content: Ulster begin what they hope will be a block of fixtures that will take then into June this Friday night when they travel to Edinburgh in a rearranged URC fixture postponed last October due to Storm Ciara. Still very much in the hunt for league and EPCR Challenge Cup honours, it’s exciting times for the Belfast based club after their much-documented struggles last term. Last season was one to forget for Ulster, but in defeat, their two games with eventual Champions Cup winners Bordeaux Bégles was a prime example of what Ulster could do when they clicked. Ultimately, Ulster’s play-off challenged fizzled out, finishing 14th in the URC rankings. But with a stronger mindset, and a drive to improve with every passing game, Michael Lowry is confident that last year was simply a one-off. “I think we never want it to happen again,” said Lowry on last season’s poor return. “I think our mindset’s just to get better, keep getting better every week and every day and like you can talk about last season, but there was games in that season that were very fine margins as well. “I suppose we’ve just changed certain elements of our game. We’ve really focused on the elements that matter. We’re getting into better positions; we’re finishing off well definitely the start of season anyway we’re finishing off those opportunities much better and to give us you know a certain lead and then we can go on and really put the foot on the throat in those games. “So, like the season’s just been so exciting. I think you know like I’ve obviously not been a part of a couple of games and I’ve just been loving watching how we’re attacking the game and our intent in games and it’s just great to see the differences I suppose.” Ulster are strongly fancied to make the play-offs and they are right in the mix for a top four finish which secure a home quarter-final. There is also the possibility of a home run of games in the EPCR Challenge Cup if they can keep their unbeaten home form intact. But Lowry isn’t allowing himself to get too carried away. Instead, the aim to improve and learn from some recent defeats on the road. “To be honest, you can’t get too far ahead of yourselves and like we’re not where we want to be yet. Like we’re constantly still improving, we’ve lost a couple of away games that we feel like we should win you know and in those play-off games that’s what’s going to happen. So, we need to keep getting way better so that we can put those games to bed. “Yes, there’s plenty of...

title: Multiple party membership to attract N10m, 2 years jail term as Reps amends Electoral Act
content: The House of Representatives on Wednesday, March 11, amended the Electoral Act 2026 to criminalise any form of multiple political party membership. With the newly amended law, any individual who is found guilty of belonging to more than one political party would be liable to get a fine of N10 million and a maximum jail term of two years. On the floor of the plenary, the House of Representatives had introduced three different new subsections to section 77 of the Electoral Act 2026. The section provides conditiona for which an individual can belong to a political party. However, with the new amendment to the Act, anyone with more than one membership can be said not to belong to any party. The new subsection reads, “A person shall not be registered as a member of more than one political party at the same time. “Where it is established that a person is registered as a member of more than one political party at the same time, such dual membership shall be void, and the person shall cease to be recognised as a valid member of any political party pending regularisation in accordance with the provisions of this Act and the constitution of the political party concerned,” it stated. In addition, it also noted that any person who intentionally registers his or her membership with multiple political parties commits a crime. “A person who knowingly registers or maintains membership in more than one political party at the same time commits an offence and is liable on conviction to a fine of N10,000,000 or to imprisonment for a term of two years, or both,” it added. The new amendment by the Lower Chamber is anticipated to aid in strengthening the legal framework governing party affiliation. It is also expected to reduce the cases of dual or multiple political party membership among Nigerians and politicians alike. The post Multiple party membership to attract N10m, 2 years jail term as Reps amends Electoral Act appeared first on Politics Nigeria .
creator: mojeed oladipupo

He said the war’s goals include enabling the Iranian people to take hold of their future in what he called a once-in-a-generation opportunity. An Israeli intelligence assessment indicates that Iran’s new supreme leader also was wounded at the start of the war. Here is the latest: Iran questions World Cup participation The head of Iran’s Football Federation raised doubts about whether Iran will take part in the World Cup that will be held across North America in June, after several members of the Iranian women’s soccer team sought asylum in Australia. Mehdi Taj told Iranian state media in an interview broadcast on Tuesday that members of the women’s soccer were coerced into staying in Australia, and accused Trump of interfering, referring to the soccer players as “hostages.” Iran is scheduled to play its first match against New Zealand on June 16 in Los Angeles. “If the outlook for the World Cup is like this, no reasonable person would accept to send their national team to such a place,” he said. Israeli intelligence assessment indicates Iran’s new Supreme Leader was injured at start of the war That’s according to an Israeli intelligence official and a reservist with knowledge of the situation. They spoke on condition of anonymity because they were not authorized to discuss the matter with the media. They gave no details on the nature of Ayatollah Motjaba Khamenei’s injuries. He said the war’s goals include enabling the Iranian people to take hold of their future in what he called a once-in-a-generation opportunity. He acknowledged that people could risk death if they rise up, but said “freedom has a price. People pay. Sometimes with their life.” “What Israel and the United States have done together is reduce the cost of freedom for the Iranian people,” he said. “It’s time to leave this evil death cult regime behind.” When asked in the interview if there’s a plan B if that doesn’t transpire, Eisenberg said: “Plan B is not on the table right now.” Putin speaks with the UAE's leader Russia’s President Vladimir Putin spoke about the war Wednesday with the United Arab Emirates president, Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan. title: The Latest: Global energy concerns mount as Iran threats stop cargo through the Strait of Hormuz
content: The ongoing Israeli-American war on Iran, for all its complexity and global effects, boils down to a single question: Who can take the pain the longest? A surge in oil prices points to what may be Iran’s most effective weapon and the United States’ biggest vulnerability in continuing the campaign: Damaging the world economy. Wednesday’s major developments include Iranian attacks against commercial ships across the Persian Gulf and its targeting of Dubai International Airport, escalating a campaign of squeezing the oil-rich region as global energy concerns mount. The U.S. campaign of air strikes also continues, and the Israeli military said it is simultaneously striking across Iran as well as Hezbollah infrastructure in Beirut. title: The Latest: Global energy concerns mount as Iran threats stop cargo through the Strait of Hormuz
content: The ongoing American-Israeli war on Iran, for all its complexity and global effects, boils down to a single question: Who can take the pain the longest? A surge in oil prices points to what may be Iran’s most effective weapon and the United States’ biggest vulnerability in continuing the campaign: Damaging the world economy. Wednesday’s major developments include Iranian attacks against commercial ships across the Persian Gulf and its targeting of Dubai International Airport, escalating a campaign of squeezing the oil-rich region as global energy concerns mount. The U.S. campaign of air strikes also continues, and the Israeli military said it is simultaneously striking across Iran as well as what it says is Hezbollah infrastructure in Lebanon. An Israeli intelligence assessment indicates that Iran’s new supreme leader also was wounded at the start of the war. The war has killed at least 1,230 people in Iran, at least 570 in Lebanon and 12 in Israel, according to officials in those countries. The Pentagon said Tuesday that about 140 U.S. service members have been wounded, eight of them severely, and seven have been killed. Here is the latest: Shrapnel punches through several floors of a Palestinian family home in shelter-less West Bank Imad Darwish of Biddya — a northern West Bank town of more than 10,000 people — told The Associated Press that his two nieces panicked as the debris tore through concrete, sparking a small fire fueled by rocket propellant. “Thankfully there were no injuries, only minor panic,” Darwish said. He had been at nightly Ramadan prayers at the time. The Israeli-occupied West Bank does not have the same siren and shelter system Israelis rely on, although warning sirens from nearby Israeli settlements can often be heard. No Palestinians have been killed by Iranian missiles or falling debris from Israeli interceptions, but large fragments have hit multiple homes. Palestinian Civil Defense crews arrived to remove the debris from the Darwish’s ceiling and extinguish the fire. Trump keeps telling America he’s winning in Iran. He’s less clear in explaining how the war ends Facing jittery global markets and drooping poll numbers since launching a war on Iran, President Donald Trump has cycled from calls for “unconditional surrender” to sounding amenable to an end state in which Iran trades one hard-line ayatollah for another. title: La exhibición más salvaje de la NBA: 83 puntos de Adebayo
content: El pívot de Miami, Bam Adebayo, ha hecho historia en la NBA. El motivo es que Adebayo ha firmado 83 puntos asombrando al mundo del baloncesto, cifra que además consigue que supere una de las marcas más legendarias de la liga: los 81 puntos que Kobe Bryant anotó en 2006.No solo eso, sino que, tras esta última actuación, el jugador de 28 años se queda a solo 17 puntos del récord absoluto de la NBA, los 100 puntos que Wilt Chamberlain logró en 1962.La noticia también es que Adebayo dominó el partido desde el primer minuto y terminó el encuentro con una línea estadística espectacular: 20 de 43 en tiros de campo, 7 de 22 en triples, 36 de 43 en tiros libres, 9 rebotes, 3 asistencias, 2 robos y 2 tapones.Pero no solo eso, sino que también estableció el récord de la NBA tanto en tiros libres anotados (36) como intentados (43) en un mismo partido. Su progresión durante el encuentro fue constante: 31 puntos en el primer cuarto, 43 al descanso y 62 al final del tercer periodo.Un final entre cánticos de MVP 💥 HISTÓRICO 💥 BAM ADEBAYO HA ANOTADO 83 PUNTOS, LA 2o MAYOR ANOTACIÓN EN LA HISTORIA DE LA NBA: 13/21 en TC 7/22 en T3 36/43 en TL Una actuación para la historia 🤯 pic.twitter.com/iwkavAwI9N — Pasion Basket (@PasionBasketNBA) March 11, 2026 head.load({"twitter":"https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js"}); A falta de poco más de un minuto para el final, el pívot abandonó la pista entre ovaciones del público y gritos de "MVP". Pero es que además, el técnico de los Heat, Erik Spoelstra, calificó la actuación de su jugador como una noche "absolutamente surrealista". El propio Adebayo también se mostró sorprendido por lo ocurrido. "Wilt, yo, luego Kobe... suena a locura", reconoció tras el partido.No debemos pasar tampoco por alto que la actuación tuvo un componente emocional para el jugador... Tanto que Adebayo terminó el encuentro visiblemente emocionado y abrazando a su madre en la pista.El pívot explicó que su objetivo era simplemente mantenerse concentrado y aprovechar el momento. "No pensé que serían 83 puntos. Vivir algo así delante de mi familia y de la afición es surrealista".Reacciones en la NBALa histórica actuación provocó reacciones inmediatas en toda la liga. Por ejemplo, uno de los primeros en reaccionar fue LeBron James, que tenía el récord anotador de los Heat con 61 puntos, celebrando el logro en redes sociales.También Kevin Durant destacó la dificultad física de una actuación de este tipo, señalando...

title: This is how much airfare could go up by amid soaring costs due to Iran war
content: SASKATOON - Canadians looking to book flights in the coming weeks and months could see a noticeable spike in airfare, as the war in Iran propels the price of jet fuel. John Gradek, an aviation management lecturer at McGill University, estimates international flights could increase by $100 to $200 one way, if jet fuel prices remain high. “Expect fare increases and significant fare increases over the next few months,” Gradek told CTV News. “If you want to buy a ticket next week, the fuel surcharges will be there. If you buy travel for July or August, yes, those surcharges will be there.” He’s unsure how domestic travel will be impacted as airlines could try to minimize the effects within North America. If nothing can be done, he suspects a fuel surcharge between $50 and $100 will likely be added onto Canadian and U.S. flights. In a statement to CTV News, West Jet said fuel is the largest input cost for an airline and the abrupt increase in jet fuel costs has already made operating flights more expensive. “Based on this, it’s likely further pricing adjustments may be needed,” the company said. A spokesperson for Porter Airlines said it’s too early to forecast how ticket prices may be impacted. Air Canada also wouldn’t speculate on future prices but said the airline has taken “hedging positions for a small portion of our short-term needs to manage fuel price volatility for already booked revenues.” Price of oil zigzags According to Google Flights Price Tracking, a one-way ticket from Saskatoon to Toronto in May cost about $137 a week ago. That same flight now costs around $226. Similarly, an international flight in May from Saskatoon to London, England cost $563 last week. This week it costs $649. It’s not clear if soaring oil prices are behind those specific increases. Earlier in the week, the price of oil zigzagged up and down, but remained more than US$90/barrel, about 40 per cent more than airlines accounted for in their budgets, according to Gradek. “No matter where the number settles, it’s going to be higher than what the airlines had planned to spend in 2026 in terms of their budgets and the basis for their airfares,” he said. On Tuesday, Scandinavian Airlines along with airlines in Australia and New Zealand announced airfare hikes due to the spike in fuel costs driven by the conflict in the Middle East. Robert Kokonis, president of Toronto-based aviation advisory AirTrav Inc., believes it’s too early to tell how the conflict will impact airfare in...
creator: allison bamford

title: Kangana Ranaut to play nurse in 26/11-based film Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata: Report
content: Actor Kangana Ranaut is set to headline another film inspired by real events. Her upcoming project, tentatively titled Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata, will reportedly revisit the incident at Cama and Albless Hospital during the 2008 Mumbai attacks. Kangana Ranaut to play nurse in 26/11-based film Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata: ReportAccording to a report by Variety India, the film will centre on the dramatic events that unfolded at the hospital on November 26, 2008, when two Pakistani terrorists, Ajmal Kasab and Ismail Khan, stormed the premises. During the attack, guards were killed and hospital staff members were injured as the assailants attempted to take control of the building. Despite the threat, hospital workers acted swiftly to protect patients. Doors and windows were shut, lights were switched off and mobile phones were turned off to avoid detection. Staff members also moved patients to safer areas of the building, continuing their efforts to safeguard lives even as the situation remained uncertain. The hospital soon became a key location during the initial hours of the attacks, as a fierce shootout took place between the terrorists and the Mumbai Police in the surrounding area. Story to focus on hospital workers Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata will chronicle these events with a focus on the hospital’s blue-collar staff and their efforts to protect patients during the crisis. The film aims to highlight the courage of everyday workers who played a critical role behind the scenes. Kangana Ranaut is expected to portray a staff nurse in the film. Actor Girija Oak will also play a prominent role. Oak is known for her performances in films such as Shor in the City, Qala, Jawan and Inspector Zende. The project is being directed by Manoj Tapadia, who began his career as a copywriter and later worked on dialogues and songs before moving into advertising. The film marks his feature directorial debut. The makers describe the project as a “cinematic tribute to the unsung heroes.” Shooting has already been completed and the film is currently in post-production, with a release planned for late 2026. Up next, Ranaut will appear alongside R. Madhavan in an upcoming thriller directed by A. L. Vijay. She is also reportedly in discussions with Aanand L. Rai for Tanu Weds Manu 3, although the project has yet to be officially confirmed. Cancel any time. To continue reading, please subscribe: Add Free Press access to your Brandon Sun subscription for only an additional $1 for the first 4 weeks* CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Adrian Autry has been fired as head basketball coach at Syracuse after three largely unsuccessful seasons. The school announced the decision Wednesday, one day after the Orange lost 86-69 to SMU in the first round of the Atlantic Coast Conference Tournament. Syracuse lost its final six games and 12 of the last 15 under Autry to finish 15-17. Autry was 49-48 over three seasons after replacing Jim Boeheim and failed to reach the NCAA Tournament. “Adrian first came to Syracuse as a student-athlete in 1990, and this program has been a constant in his life ever since: as a player, assistant coach, associate head coach and ultimately as head coach,” Syracuse athletic director John Wildhack said in a news release. “His dedication to our student-athletes on and off the court never wavered throughout his time here, and we are grateful for his service and commitment to Orange Basketball.” Autry said Tuesday after the loss that “ I didn’t get the results that we wanted.” “It has been an honor to coach at my alma mater, ” Autry said in a statement Wednesday following the firing. “I want to thank Chancellor Syverud, John Wildhack, Jim Boeheim, my team and my staff for their support.” The four-year Syracuse starter under Jim Boeheim and later his associate head coach took over for the retiring Hall of Famer in 2023, only to fail to gain any traction in carrying the program into its post-Boeheim era. Last spring, Wildhack didn’t lay out specifically what Autry had to do to keep his job. He did, however, lay out clear expectations: “The goal of this program is we should be playing meaningful games in March.” Wildhack already has announced he is retiring in July, and replacing Autry will...

content: By Canadian Press on March 11, 2026. Orange lost 86-69 to SMU in the first round of the Atlantic Coast Conference Tournament. Syracuse lost its final six games and 12 of the last 15 under Autry to finish 15-17. Autry was 49-48 over three seasons after replacing Jim Boeheim and failed to reach the NCAA Tournament. “Adrian first came to Syracuse as a student-athlete in 1990, and this program has been a constant in his life ever since: as a player, assistant coach, associate head coach and ultimately as head coach,” Syracuse athletic director John Wildhack said in a news release. “His dedication to our student-athletes on and off the court never wavered throughout his time here, and we are grateful for his service and commitment to Orange Basketball.” Autry said Tuesday after the loss that “ I didn’t get the results that we wanted.” “It has been an honor to coach at my alma mater, ” Autry said in a statement Wednesday following the firing. “I want to thank Chancellor Syverud, John Wildhack, Jim Boeheim, my team and my staff for their support.” The four-year Syracuse starter under Jim Boeheim and later his associate head coach took over for the retiring Hall of Famer in 2023, only to fail to gain any traction in carrying the program into its post-Boeheim era. Last spring, Wildhack didn’t lay out specifically what Autry had to do to keep his job. He did, however, lay out clear expectations: “The goal of this program is we should be playing meaningful games in March.” Wildhack already has announced he is retiring in July, and replacing Autry will be among his final responsibilities. A national search will begin immediately. Autry failed to get the Orange to March Madness, the place where Boeheim routinely led them while building a nationally relevant program. Instead, Syracuse finished with consecutive losing seasons for the first time since 1968-69. Going back to NCAA Tournament expansion to 64 teams in 1985, Syracuse won the 2003 championship, reached the finals in 1987 and 1996, reached two other Final Fours in 2013 and 2016, and reached the second weekend 11 other times under Boeheim. Yet this year marks the fifth straight season without a bid, continuing a string of mediocrity across Boeheim’s final two years at the helm. Autry was 24-34 in league regular-season play in his three seasons. The Orange have lost 27 games by double-digit margins while managing just four Quadrant 1 wins that top a postseason résumé. The nadir for Autry came on Feb. 16 with a...

title: RCB, SRH clash in IPL 2026 opener: Phase 1 schedule
content: The schedule for the first phase of the 2026 Indian Premier League (IPL) was announced on Wednesday. The tournament will kick off with a blockbuster game between defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Sunrisers Hyderabad at the iconic M Chinnaswamy Stadium on March 28. Five-time champions Mumbai Indians face Kolkata Knight Riders at Wankhede Stadium the next day. As reported earlier, only the schedule until April 12 has been announced so far. On Tuesday, BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia told IANS, We plan to announce the IPL 2026 schedule by March 12. For now, we are going to announce the IPL schedule for the first 20 days. The remaining fixtures will be announced later as the BCCI plans to avoid any clash with assembly elections in West Bengal, Assam, and Tamil Nadu. As mentioned, the opening match will be held at M Chinnaswamy Stadium, which was earlier under fire for the 2025 stampede incident that left 11 people dead and several injured. Although the Karnataka government gave the stadium the required clearance to host the IPL matches, it is subject to clearance from an expert committee constituted by the Karnataka state government, as per an IPL release. As per the schedule announced, the first phase will have 20 matches, including four double-headers on April 4-5 and 11-12. All 10 teams have been assigned four matches each in this phase. Notably, Rajasthan Royals will play three home games in Guwahati and four others at Jaipur's Sawai Mansingh Stadium. Similarly, RCB will play two matches in Raipur, with Dharamsala hosting three games of Punjab Kings.
creator: parth dhall

title: Things to see and do in the Azores
content: The Azores, a group of nine volcanic islands in the mid-Atlantic, are a hidden gem for nature lovers. These islands are famous for their lush landscapes, unique wildlife, and dramatic geological formations. With mild weather and stunning views, the Azores make for an ideal destination for those seeking tranquility away from the hustle and bustle of city life. Here's a guide to exploring this secret paradise. The Azores are famous for their volcanic origins, which have shaped the islands' unique landscapes. From craters to hot springs, the islands have a lot to offer. You can visit Sete Cidades on Sao Miguel Island, which has two lakes in a caldera. Furnas Valley has geothermal activity with hot springs and fumaroles. The dramatic cliffs of Pico Island give stunning views of the Atlantic Ocean. The verdant vegetation of the Azores is a result of its mild climate and fertile soil. The islands are home to several endemic plant species, making them a botanist's dream destination. Hiking trails snake through dense forests and offer breathtaking views at every turn. One can explore Terra Nostra Garden in Furnas or hike along the trails of Corvo Island's Caldeirao crater. The Azores are home to a variety of wildlife, both on land and in the sea. Birdwatchers will be delighted to find several endemic species, including the Azorean Bullfinch and Trocaz Pigeon. The waters around the islands are also teeming with marine life, including dolphins and whales that can be seen during boat tours or whale-watching excursions. The Azorean culture is a mix of Portuguese traditions and local influences, which is evident in its cuisine. The islands are famous for their dairy products, especially cheese from Sao Jorge Island. You can also try local delicacies such as cozido das furnas, a stew cooked underground using volcanic heat. Visiting local markets gives you a taste of traditional crafts and fresh produce grown on these fertile islands.
creator: simran jeet

title: Switzerland's most beautiful lakeside towns: A list
content: Switzerland's mountain lakeside towns are the perfect places to escape the hustle and bustle of city life. These towns, set against stunning landscapes, offer a peaceful retreat for those looking to relax. Be it the calm of the water or the majesty of mountains, these places have it all. Here are some of the best Swiss mountain lakeside towns for a peaceful getaway. Interlaken is situated between Lake Thun and Lake Brienz, making it a perfect base for exploring the Bernese Oberland region. The town offers a range of outdoor activities, from hiking to boating. Visitors can enjoy scenic train rides to nearby attractions such as Jungfraujoch or Schilthorn. Interlaken's central location makes it easy to explore other Swiss destinations while providing a tranquil environment for relaxation. Lucerne is famous for its preserved medieval architecture and stunning lake views. The town is located on the shores of Lake Lucerne, with options for boat tours and swimming in the summer months. Tourists can visit cultural landmarks such as Chapel Bridge and Lion Monument, or take day trips to nearby mountains like Pilatus or Rigi. Lucerne's combination of culture and nature makes it an ideal peaceful retreat. Montreux is located on Lake Geneva's shores and is famous for its mild climate and beautiful flower-filled promenades. The town is famous for its annual jazz festival but offers plenty of activities throughout the year, such as visiting Chillon Castle or hiking in the nearby Alps. Montreux's Mediterranean-like atmosphere makes it a unique destination in Switzerland. Zermatt is a car-free village at the foot of the iconic Matterhorn mountain. It offers stunning views along with a range of winter sports in the colder months and hiking trails in the warmer months. The village has charming wooden chalets that add to its alpine charm. Zermatt's unique setting makes it a peaceful getaway for nature lovers and adventure seekers alike.
creator: simran jeet

title: Hikers, Ireland has some breathtaking trails
content: Ireland's hiking trails are famous for their stunning landscapes and rich history. While some trails attract thousands of tourists, there are hidden gems that remain less explored. These lesser-known paths offer unique experiences, away from the crowds. From rugged coastlines to serene woodlands, these trails give you a chance to connect with nature in a more intimate way. Here are some of Ireland's best-kept hiking secrets. Nestled in County Wicklow, Glendalough Valley is famous for its stunning scenery and historical significance. The valley has a number of trails that wind through ancient woodlands and past the ruins of a sixth-century monastic settlement. Hikers can choose from easy walks to more strenuous hikes, all offering breathtaking views of the surrounding mountains and lakes. The Burren Way takes you through one of Ireland's most unique geological formations in County Clare. This trail offers hikers a chance to explore limestone pavements dotted with rare flora and fauna. The route also takes you through quaint villages where you can experience traditional Irish culture. The Burren Way is perfect for those looking for both natural beauty and cultural immersion. The Mourne Mountains in Northern Ireland are famous for their dramatic peaks and sweeping vistas. The mountains offer a range of hiking options, from gentle walks to challenging climbs. The area is also home to some of Ireland's highest peaks, making it a favorite among experienced hikers seeking adventure in a stunning setting. The Causeway Coastal Route is a scenic drive that also offers some amazing hiking opportunities along Northern Ireland's coastline. This trail takes you past iconic landmarks such as Giant's Causeway and Carrick-a-Rede Rope Bridge, while providing stunning views of rugged cliffs and sandy beaches. It's perfect for those who want coastal scenery without venturing too far off the beaten path. Connemara National Park in County Galway is a paradise for nature lovers. With its vast expanses of peat bogs, mountains, and woodlands, it offers a variety of trails to explore. From short walks to longer treks, the park has something for everyone. It promises an immersive experience in one of Ireland's most beautiful landscapes.
creator: simran jeet

title: Photographers, Finland’s winter landscapes will amaze you
content: Finland's winter landscapes are a dream come true for photographers, with their stunning natural beauty and unique light conditions. From the Northern Lights to the snow-covered forests, Finland offers an array of magical experiences that can be captured through the lens. Here are five of them that highlight how diverse and enchanting Finland's winter scenery can be. The Northern Lights, or Aurora Borealis, are best seen in Finland's Lapland region. The phenomenon is best observed from September to March when the skies are dark and clear. Rovaniemi and Saariselka are popular spots for photographers hoping to capture this natural light display. The vibrant colors of green, purple, and red dancing across the sky make for breathtaking photographs. Finland's forests turn into a winter wonderland, blanketed in thick layers of snow. These serene landscapes are perfect for photographers looking to capture the quiet beauty of nature. The contrast between the white snow and dark tree trunks creates stunning compositions. Areas like Nuuksio National Park near Helsinki offer accessible trails for those wanting to explore these enchanting scenes. Frozen lakes in Finland provide an incredible opportunity to capture unique reflections and textures on ice. Lake Saimaa, one of the largest lakes in Finland, freezes over during winter months, providing endless possibilities for creative photography. The ice formations change daily with temperature fluctuations, giving photographers new perspectives each time they visit. Throughout Finland, ice castles and sculptures are erected every winter, showcasing the talent of artists from across the globe. These temporary structures, built entirely out of ice and snow, are a sight to behold, especially when illuminated by colorful lights at night. The Icehotel in Kemi is famous for its elaborate designs, which make for stunning photographs. Reindeer sledding adventures offer a unique way to experience Finland's winter landscapes while capturing them on camera. In Lapland, visitors can join guided tours through snowy trails pulled by reindeer teams. These excursions provide opportunities not only to photograph the animals but also to capture the stunning backdrops of pristine wilderness that surrounds them.
creator: simran jeet

title: Birdwatching in Portugal: 5 must-visit destinations
content: Southern Portugal is a birdwatcher's paradise, with its diverse habitats and colorful avian species. From coastal wetlands to rolling hills, the region has plenty of opportunities to spot some of the most colorful birds. Whether you're a seasoned ornithologist or a casual nature lover, exploring these vibrant destinations can be an unforgettable experience. Here are some of the best places in southern Portugal to witness its avian wonders. Ria Formosa Natural Park is famous for its diverse ecosystems and rich birdlife. The park has lagoons, marshes, and islands that are home to various species. Birdwatchers can spot flamingos, herons, and a number of migratory birds here. The park's well-maintained trails make it easy to explore different habitats and get close to the birds without disturbing them. The Costa Vicentina Alentejo Coast Natural Park features dramatic cliffs and pristine beaches, making it an ideal spot for birdwatching. The park is home to several seabirds such as puffins and gulls that nest along the cliffs. The coastal winds create perfect conditions for these birds, making it a great place for birdwatchers to observe their behavior in natural surroundings. Serra da Arrabida Natural Park is famous for its lush greenery and mountainous terrain. The park is home to a variety of birds, including eagles and vultures, which are often seen soaring over the hills. The diverse habitats within the park attract different species throughout the year, making it a popular spot for birdwatchers looking to see both resident and migratory birds. The Algarve region has several wetlands that serve as vital stopover points on migratory routes across Europe and Africa. These wetlands attract hundreds of thousands of birds every year, including storks, cranes, and waders like sandpipers or plovers, which feed on abundant food sources here. Birdwatching hides allow visitors an unobtrusive view into these bustling avian communities, without causing disturbance. Tavira Island is a part of Ria Formosa but deserves a special mention because of its unique offerings. The island offers pristine beaches with crystal-clear waters, where you can see colorful kingfishers diving into the sea. The surrounding dunes provide shelter to nesting seabirds during the breeding season, making it an ideal spot for those wanting to combine relaxation with some serious birdwatching.

title: Vissel Kobe beats FC Seoul, becomes 2nd Japanese team in AFC Champions League quarterfinals
content: Japan’s Vissel Kobe claimed a place in the quarterfinals of the AFC Champions League Elite with a 2-1 win over FC Seoul of South Korea on Wednesday. Yuya Osako and Yosuke Ideguchi scored late at home as Kobe advanced 3-1 over two legs in the round of 16. Patryk Klimala scored in the first half for Seoul. Kobe becomes the second Japanese team to reach the quarterfinals after Machida Zelvia qualified on Tuesday. Johor Darul Tazim of Malaysia also advanced despite a 1-0 loss at Sanfrecce Hiroshima, thanks to its 3-1 win in the first leg, to prevent a third Japanese reaching the last eight. Buriram United of Thailand rounds out the eastern zone quarterfinalists. The tournament is divided into two geographic zones until the quarterfinals. Games in the western zone have been postponed because of the Iran war. All matches from the quarterfinal stage onward are scheduled to take place in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, from April 16–25 but it’s unclear when games in the western zone can resume. ___ AP soccer: https://apnews.com/hub/soccer The Associated Press

title: IEA agrees to record release of emergency oil reserves in an effort to calm surging prices
content: BERLIN (AP) — The International Energy Agency agreed Wednesday to release the largest volume of emergency oil reserves in its history, in a bid to counter the effects on energy markets of the war in the Middle East. The Paris-based organization said it will make 400 million barrels of oil available from its members’ emergency reserves. It’s a larger stock than the 182.7 million barrels that were released in 2022 by the IEA's 32 member countries in response to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. IEA member countries currently hold over 1.2 billion barrels of public emergency oil stocks, with a further 600 million barrels of industry stocks held under government obligation. In response to US and Israel strikes, Iran has attacked commercial ships across the Persian Gulf, escalating a campaign of squeezing the oil-rich region as global energy concerns mount. Iran has effectively stopped cargo traffic in the narrow Hormuz Strait through which about a fifth of all oil is shipped from the Persian Gulf toward the Indian Ocean. It has also targeted oil fields and refineries in Gulf Arab nations, aiming at generating enough global economic pain to pressure the United States and Israel to end their strikes. G7 Energy ministers on Tuesday said they supported in principle “the implementation of proactive measures to address the situation, including the use of strategic reserves." According to the IEA, export volumes of crude and refined products are currently at less than 10% of pre-war levels. THIS IS A BREAKING NEWS UPDATE. AP’s earlier story follows below. BERLIN (AP) — Germany and Austria said Wednesday they are releasing parts of their oil reserves following an International Energy Agency request for members to release a record 400 million barrels to help temper energy price spikes due to the Iran war. Japan also said it will release some of its reserves starting Monday. Group of Seven energy ministers met Tuesday at IEA headquarters in Paris to look at ways to bring down prices that have risen because of the Iran war. IEA executive director Fatih Birol said afterward that they discussed all available options, including making IEA emergency oil stocks available to the market. The largest-ever previous collective release of emergency stocks by IEA member countries was 182.7 million barrels, in the wake of the energy shock prompted by Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022. The reserves were established in 1974 following the Arab oil embargo. IEA members...
creator: kirsten grieshaber and samuel petrequin
